output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Demand It Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and get your money back fast with Deposits-Back.com. No signup needed.
content_outline: The Illegal Moves Landlords Use to Keep Your Deposit
What State Laws Actually Say About Deposit Deadlines
How to Demand Your Deposit Back in 60 Seconds (No Lawyer Needed)
key_points: Most states require landlords to return deposits within 14-30 days with an itemized list of deductions
Normal wear and tear cannot be legally deducted from your security deposit
A formal demand letter is often the fastest way to force repayment before filing in small claims court
Deposits-Back.com generates state-specific demand letters in 60 seconds for $19.99 with no sign-up or subscription required
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds. Just $19.99. No sign-ups. No subscriptions. Get your money back now.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are wrongfully withholding, deducting from, or refusing to return a security deposit and need a fast, affordable legal solution without hiring an attorney
tiktok_hooks: POV: You moved out 3 months ago and your landlord is still 'processing' your $2,000 deposit...
My landlord tried to charge me $800 for 'cleaning' a spotless apartment. So I did this.
They legally cannot keep your deposit for this. 🛑 #renting #tenantrights
If your landlord hasn't returned your deposit in 30 days, they're banking on you not knowing this.
Stop asking your landlord for your deposit back. Start demanding it. 👇

short_form_scripts.name: The Deadline Script
lines: Your landlord has 21 to 30 days. That's it.
If they miss the deadline, they often can't deduct a single penny.
You don't need a lawyer. You need a demand letter.
Deposits-Back.com. 60 seconds. $19.99. No signup. Link in bio.

short_form_scripts.name: Normal Wear and Tear Script
lines: Faded paint. Carpet wear. Nail holes.
Your landlord cannot deduct 'normal wear and tear' from your deposit. It's the law.
But they will if you don't call them out officially.
Send a demand letter in 60 seconds at Deposits-Back.com. No subscription.
